trivia_bullets.text: Fowl Strike is a common green instant in Modern Horizons 3 (MH3, 2024-06-14), a direct-to-Modern set bypassing Standard legality. Legal in Modern, Legacy, Pauper, and Commander; not legal in Standard or Pioneer.
sources: Scryfall: set_type draft_innovation and legalities fields

trivia_bullets.text: "With a resounding wallop, the ancient battle between buzzard and druid was finally finished." The flavor text frames the art's combat as the conclusion of a long-running conflict, lending absurdist gravity to a simple removal spell — 'ancient' and 'finally' imply this specific bird-vs-druid rivalry has deep multiverse history.

Destroys target creature with flying for {1}{G}. Reinforce 2 ({2}{G}, discard this card: put two +1/+1 counters on target creature) provides an alternative when anti-air removal isn't needed. Reinforce was introduced in Morningtide (2008, Lorwyn block) and is considered by Wizards R&D to have been poorly executed at the card-by-card level in its original printing; its MH3 reuse reflects retooling for direct-to-Modern utility.
sources: WebSearch: Lorwyn Eclipsed mechanics article — Reinforce noted as underperforming in original Morningtide block

trivia_bullets.text: EDHREC rank 19,460 for MH3. Situationally useful in formats with heavy flying threats; Reinforce 2 ensures the card is never dead in hand.
Illustrated by Warren Mahy, a New Zealand-based concept artist with a substantial MTG portfolio spanning creature and action pieces across many sets.

related_cards.name: Plummet
related_cards.note: The baseline {1}{G} anti-flying benchmark — Fowl Strike is Plummet plus Reinforce 2 at the same cost

related_cards.name: Giant Killer
related_cards.note: Another common that handles flying creatures; illustrates the design space for anti-air commons

related_cards.name: Bonesplitter
related_cards.note: Classic equipment with +1/+1 counter adjacency; contextualizes Reinforce as a design axis in aggressive decks
